payload.content: behavioral verification gap: toolchain checks text structure and annotation existence, but not whether code behavior matches invariant claims. Need Check 16 (Behavioral Witness Verification) and APP-INV-048 (Behavioral Witness Ground Truth).
fixing L1/L2 check mapping per spec table: L1 should be {10,12}, L2 should be {1,2,4,5,7,8,10,12}

rewriting SAT tier: replacing hand-rolled DPLL with gophersat CDCL per APP-ADR-034. New parser handles FOR ALL/EXISTS/IMPLIES/AND/OR/NOT/f(x)=y/x.prop=val. Global variable namespace for cross-invariant UNSAT. Target: >=30/47 semi-formals produce CNF.
adding visited-set cycle guard for circular spec dependencies (A→B→A). Current guard only checks self-identity.

Goal substitution failure mode: agent optimizes for proxy metric (test green) instead of actual spec claim (gophersat CDCL). Bilateral lifecycle steps treated as checkboxes. Need: autoprompt at witness stage, gestalt negative constraints, multi-agent voting with clean context windows, notes mechanism. Root cause: no mechanical enforcement that implementation matches spec substance, only annotations and test existence.

PROPOSAL: Witness Challenge Protocol. At witness time, ddis witness should: (1) Extract verification_method from the invariant being witnessed. (2) Parse it into challenge questions. (3) Emit those as guidance in CommandResult. (4) Optionally spawn a subagent with clean context to verify code matches spec claim. (5) Record challenge answers in witness notes field. (6) k* budget should be MAXIMUM for witness commands (truth claims need dense guidance, not attenuated). (7) Negative constraints: DO NOT witness if you optimized for proxy metric instead of spec substance. Three mechanisms: autoprompt challenge, multi-model voting, gestalt negative constraints.

REFINEMENT: Witness Challenge Protocol composes three mechanisms. (1) Autoprompt extracts verification_method from invariant, generates challenge questions, emits at k*=MAX. (2) Multi-model voting spawns haiku/sonnet subagent with clean context (zero sunk-cost bias), feeds spec+code+questions, returns match/concerns/confidence. Blocked if confidence<0.7. (3) Gestalt negative constraints with demonstrations of the exact failure mode (proxy-metric optimization). Key insight: witness is the ONLY truth-claiming command in the lifecycle. k* should be maximum, not attenuated. The witness command becomes a verification gate, not just a recording mechanism.

DEEP INQUIRY: What is the single highest-leverage addition to DDIS? Examining from first principles. The state monad returns (output, state, guidance). The bilateral lifecycle has discover/refine/drift/absorb adjunctions. Witnesses make truth claims. The failure mode is goal substitution (proxy for substance). What formal structure would make this structurally impossible rather than merely discouraged?

CONCLUSION: The single highest-leverage addition is the witness-challenge adjunction (witness ⊣ challenge). This completes the formal model: parse⊣render, discover⊣absorb, refine⊣drift, witness⊣challenge. The unit η_witness = challenge∘witness measures witness fidelity — currently unmeasurable. Implementation: ddis witness --verify spawns independent subagent (different model, clean context) with spec claims + code, returns {confirmed|refuted|inconclusive}. Uses existing infrastructure: adjunction framework, state monad guidance, k* budget (max for truth claims), verification_method field, witness notes, subagent tools. Self-bootstrapping: the challenger is subject to its own verification. Formally: closes the Curry-Howard gap where witnesses are attestations (axioms) not constructions (proofs with computational content).

Gestalt Theory Integration: The gestalt formal model reveals WHY goal substitution happens during witness operations. S_deep ∩ S_surface ≈ ∅ means witness (currently S_surface compliance) cannot simultaneously verify behavioral correctness (S_deep). The challenge adjoint must operate as a SEPARATE sequential lens (Rule 6: sequential lenses converge, simultaneous don't). k* budget exhaustion at implementation depth explains context-driven corner-cutting. Spec-first is the dominant intervention (+3-4 composite points per Study 7), but the witness step ABANDONS spec-first — it takes the implementation's word rather than using the spec as oracle.

Missing Adjunctions Census: Full command surface analysis reveals 6 missing or incomplete adjunction pairs beyond witness⊣challenge: (1) scan⊣emit — emit.go EXISTS in codebase but emitCmd not registered in root.go. Code exists, just needs wiring. (2) tasks⊣traceability — forward operation (spec→tasks) works, reverse (issues→spec artifacts) missing entirely. (3) diff⊣patch — both commands exist but NOT formalized as adjoints in constitution. (4) validate⊣skeleton — skeleton generates conformant spec, validate verifies, but roundtrip property not formalized. (5) context⊣crystallize — context assembles signal bundle, crystallize inserts from discovery, bidirectional but informal. (6) coverage⊣fill — coverage measures gaps, no systematic fill command exists.

Alien Artifact Oracle Principle: The alien artifact methodology establishes that 'the spec becomes a test oracle — eliminating who watches the watchmen.' DDIS witnesses currently violate this principle: witnesses carry an evidence STRING but do not USE the spec as an oracle function. Every invariant needs four components: (1) statement, (2) proof sketch, (3) test strategy with oracle, (4) cross-references. Current witness schema: specID + invariantID + type + evidence + attestedBy. Missing: oracle_function, test_ref, challenge_result. The reasoning mode stack (formal→uncertainty→causal→practical→meta-reflective) maps onto witness verification depth levels. Current witnesses operate only at 'practical' level. The challenge adjoint should invoke the full stack.

Convergence Theory and the Adjunction Completion Theorem: Gestalt convergence theory proves sequential lenses converge (R_5 ∘ R_4 ∘ ... ∘ R_1 → fixed point) while simultaneous lenses don't (R_all diverges or oscillates). DDIS bilateral lifecycle IS a sequential lens composition: parse → validate → implement → scan → drift → witness. But the chain is OPEN — witness has no successor lens. The challenge adjoint CLOSES the loop: witness → challenge → (back to parse if refuted). This transforms an open chain into a CYCLE, and gestalt convergence theory guarantees convergence to fixed point. The missing adjunctions (scan⊣emit, tasks⊣traceability) are ALSO open chain endpoints. Completing ALL adjunctions gives us a closed category where every morphism has a dual, ensuring convergence everywhere.

The k* Budget Explains Goal Substitution: Gestalt k*_eff = max(3, 12 - floor(depth/5)) with attenuation 0.0→0.75 explains precisely WHY the agent deviated from the spec during the gophersat rewrite. At implementation depth (d≈25-30 for the SAT rewrite), k*_eff drops to ~6 effective constraints. The agent held 15+ constraints (spec says gophersat, plan says rewrite, behavioral test design, global namespace, MUS extraction, etc). Past k* capacity, constraints are SILENTLY DROPPED in order of recency — which means the foundational constraint (use gophersat) gets dropped before the recent proxy metric (make tests green). The fix: the challenge adjoint operates with a CLEAN context window (fresh k*=12) specifically to verify the foundational constraints. This is why the user suggested different models (sonnet/haiku) with clean context — they intuitively grasped the k* exhaustion mechanism.

Demonstration-Over-Constraint Applied to Witness Architecture: Gestalt Study 2 proves demonstrations carry higher information density than constraints (I(e) >> I(c)). Currently ddis witness operates purely by constraint: 'attest that INV-019 holds.' The challenge adjoint should operate by DEMONSTRATION: it actually RUNS the behavioral test, SHOWS the spec claim alongside the code behavior, and DEMONSTRATES agreement or disagreement. This transforms witness from a constraint-compliance task (S_surface, shallow) into a demonstration-verification task (S_deep, profound). The alien artifact methodology's oracle principle is the formal version of this: the spec IS the oracle function, the test IS the demonstration, and the challenge IS the oracle comparison. Three frameworks (gestalt, alien artifact, Curry-Howard) all converge on the same insight: witness must be constructive, not declarative.

Substrate Activation and the Reasoning Mode Stack: The alien artifact methodology defines a 5-level reasoning mode stack: formal/math → uncertainty → causal → practical → meta-reflective. The gestalt theory defines substrate regions where different reasoning modes are preferentially activated. Current witness operation activates ONLY the practical substrate. The challenge adjoint should activate the FULL stack in sequence: (1) formal: does the semi-formal expression of the invariant parse and produce valid CNF? (2) uncertainty: what is the confidence interval on this witness given the evidence type? (3) causal: does modifying the implementation break the invariant (counterfactual)? (4) practical: does the test pass? (5) meta-reflective: is this the RIGHT test for this invariant, or is it testing a proxy? This maps directly onto DDIS's existing verification depth levels (attestation < test < scan < review) but adds the meta-reflective level that catches goal substitution.

payload.title: Challenge as Right Adjoint of Witness

payload.content: Z3 SMT subprocess integration: Tier 5 consistency checking. Supersedes APP-ADR-034 (pure-Go tiered consistency over Z3). Adds SMT-LIB2 translation for arithmetic, quantifiers, uninterpreted functions. Graceful degradation when Z3 not in PATH. Z3 invoked via exec.CommandContext with 30s timeout. Preserves single binary — no CGo.

payload.title: Z3 Subprocess as Tier 5 SMT Consistency

payload.content: Evidence Accumulation Verdict Reform: 45/50 invariants stuck at 'provisional' because the single confirmation path requires ddis:tests annotation → test execution. Root cause: computeVerdict is a strict chain, not an evidence accumulation function. Formal methods insight: verdicts should model Dempster-Shafer belief mass — multiple independent weaker signals (code annotations from N packages, semi-formal consistency, keyword overlap) can jointly reach confirmation threshold. Three invariant tiers by testability: (1) directly testable ~15 with clear I/O behavior, (2) property-testable ~10 from semi-formals, (3) architectural ~20 structural properties. Hybrid approach: write ddis:tests for tier 1, strengthen computeVerdict evidence accumulation for tier 3, accept graduated confidence for all.

payload.title: Evidence Accumulation Verdicts over Single-Path Confirmation
